description: The composition planning dialect. Given a natural-language request, it decomposes the work into an ordered sequence of Graffiticode language ids — a linear pipeline where each language consumes the data model produced by the next. A program is `plan ["<id>" ...]` and compiles to { langs: [...] } (one id = atomic, empty = no composition). It authors no content itself; it only decides which languages run and in what order.

example_prompts.prompt: Create a simple spreadsheet assessment for Learnosity.
example_prompts.notes: Host L0158 describes-but-does-not-author the spreadsheet, so it consumes L0166 -> plan ["0158" "0166"].

example_prompts.prompt: Make a multiple-choice question about France.
example_prompts.notes: L0158 authors MCQs directly -> single-stage plan ["0158"].
